name: yaml-authoring
description: Create and validate YAML diagram files. Use when writing new diagrams or troubleshooting YAML syntax.
capabilities: Write valid YAML for AWS architecture diagrams
Configure nodes with providers, kinds, and layouts
Nest resources inside VPC and subnet containers
Use auto-layout for automatic node positioning
author: 7nohe
securitySummary: This is a documentation-only skill containing YAML syntax examples for architecture diagrams. No executable code, scripts, network calls, or filesystem access. Static findings are false positives triggered by technical terminology and code block delimiters being misinterpreted as security threats.
valueStatement: Writing YAML for infrastructure diagrams can be error-prone with cryptic validation errors. This skill provides clear syntax guides, examples, and troubleshooting for Figram YAML diagrams so you can create AWS architecture diagrams without guessing.
limitations: Limited to AWS provider examples shown

useCases.title: Document Cloud Infrastructure
useCases.description: Create infrastructure documentation by writing simple YAML instead of dragging and dropping components in FigJam.

useCases.title: Share Architecture Designs
useCases.description: Define cloud architectures in code for version control and shareable diagrams that sync to FigJam.

useCases.title: Generate Consistent Diagrams
useCases.description: Produce standardized architecture diagrams using YAML templates that ensure visual consistency.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Diagram
promptTemplates.prompt: Help me write a Figram YAML diagram with a Lambda function connected to an S3 bucket.

promptTemplates.title: VPC Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a YAML diagram for a VPC with public subnets containing an ALB and Lambda functions.

promptTemplates.title: API with Database
promptTemplates.prompt: Write YAML for an API Gateway connected to a Lambda handler that reads from DynamoDB.

promptTemplates.title: Fix Validation Error
promptTemplates.prompt: I am getting this error: Missing required field version. Help me fix my YAML diagram.

bestPractices: Use descriptive node IDs like user-api instead of n1 for clarity
Nest resources inside VPC/Subnet containers to organize complex architectures
Child nodes can omit layout for automatic grid-based positioning
Add comments with # to document purpose of each component
antiPatterns: Using duplicate node IDs causing validation errors
Specifying only x or y coordinate without the other
Forgetting that container nodes require width and height
Not quoting labels with spaces or special characters

faq.question: What providers are supported?
faq.answer: Figram supports AWS, GCP, and Azure. Use provider: aws, gcp, or azure in node definitions.

faq.question: Can I use auto-layout for child nodes?
faq.answer: Yes. Child nodes with a parent field can omit layout entirely for automatic positioning in a grid.

faq.question: How do I nest resources inside a VPC?
faq.answer: Set the parent field on child nodes to reference the VPC or subnet node ID for automatic relative positioning.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides YAML syntax guidance. No data is sent anywhere. All processing happens locally via Figram CLI.

faq.question: Why am I getting Missing required field version?
faq.answer: Every diagram YAML must start with version: 1 and include a unique docId at the document root level.

faq.question: How is this different from drawing in FigJam directly?
faq.answer: YAML authoring lets you version control diagrams, use text editors you prefer, and generate consistent architectures without manual positioning.
